title: Jamie Oliver's Beef Short Ribs

overview.description: Slow-braised beef short ribs cooked with celery, carrots, walnuts, onion marmalade and smooth ale until the meat becomes exceptionally tender.

ingredients: 6 beef short ribs, bone in, approximately 1.6kg
method: Heat a large shallow casserole pan over medium-high heat.
Brown the short ribs thoroughly on all sides, turning regularly, for approximately 15 minutes.
Remove the ribs to a bowl.
Trim the celery and peel the carrots.
Finely slice the celery, reserving any good celery leaves for serving.
Add the celery, carrots, walnuts and onion marmalade to the casserole.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Pour in the smooth ale and 600ml water.
Return the ribs to the pan and nestle them into the liquid.
Cover with damp greaseproof paper and place in the oven.
Cook for approximately 4 hours, basting halfway through, until the meat pulls easily away from the bones.
Scrub the potatoes and prick them all over with a fork.
Bake the potatoes alongside the ribs for the final 1½ hours.
Remove excess fat from the surface of the braising liquid.
Remove the bones and any unwanted pieces of connective tissue.
Serve the tender beef with the vegetables, baked potatoes and creamed horseradish.
Scatter over the reserved celery leaves.

learning.did-you-know: Short ribs are a relatively tough cut, but long, gentle cooking transforms the connective tissue into gelatin and makes the meat exceptionally tender.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How a long braise transforms a tough cut of beef and how a combination of ale, vegetables, onion marmalade and walnuts creates a rich cooking liquid.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A memorable, comforting batch-friendly meal that makes excellent use of an inexpensive cut of beef and produces a deeply flavoured braising liquid with valuable leftovers.
chapman-tips: Brown the ribs properly; this provides much of the depth of flavour.
Don't rush the cooking time. The meat is ready when it pulls easily from the bone.
The braising liquid should not completely evaporate during cooking.
The baked potatoes are particularly good for soaking up the rich cooking liquid.
The walnuts add texture and a subtle richness to the braising mixture.
Creamed horseradish provides useful sharpness against the rich beef.
Save any leftover braising liquid rather than throwing it away.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes well. Freeze the beef and braising liquid together in portions where possible.
notes.leftovers: Excellent reheated. The leftover beef can be used in pies, sandwiches or shredded into a rich beef dish. The leftover braising liquid can be strained, skimmed and frozen for use in gravy, stews, lentils or other braises.
